focus: maintainability — complete six-packet intra-routing recall and replay/runtime parity

findingDetails.title: Four packet blind holdouts miss their declared intent
findingDetails.evidence: CU-H01, CU-H02, MB-H01, and MB-H02 each produce no deterministic intent. The other eight blind packet holdouts select their declared intent.
findingDetails.impact: ClickUp and Mobbin expose no packet-local generalization margin beyond their literal keyword fixtures.
findingDetails.recommendation: Address through a separate packet vocabulary/gold decision; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All 12 blind holdouts across the six packet packs were replayed and compared to expected_intent.

findingDetails.title: All six runtime fallbacks violate negative no-resource gold
findingDetails.evidence: Each negative gold declares none/empty. All six runtime pseudocode no-score branches load a default or hardcoded reference, and five also expose a fallback intent label.
findingDetails.impact: The packet-local suppression corpus and documented runtime behavior cannot both pass, obscuring whether out-of-domain packet invocations are rejected or serviced with fallback context.
findingDetails.recommendation: Resolve as a separate suppression-contract decision; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All six negative files and every packet's zero-score runtime branch were traced.

findingDetails.title: Deterministic replay omits runtime no-match behavior
findingDetails.evidence: Generic replay returns no intents on zero scores and derives only declared DEFAULT_RESOURCE/RESOURCE_MAP paths. Runtime routers return fallback labels; ClickUp loads a hardcoded cupt_commands path deliberately absent from DEFAULT_RESOURCE.
findingDetails.impact: The CI replay cannot validate packet runtime fallback intents or resources, so packet suppression and unknown-route results differ by execution path.
findingDetails.recommendation: Use a separate router parity remediation; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: No-score results were compared across replay and all six runtime pseudocode branches.

findingDetails.title: Eleven positive packet scenarios omit always-loaded base gold
findingDetails.evidence: Four Aside, three Chrome, two Mobbin, and two Refero positive/holdout rows omit the packet's documented universal base reference even though replay returns it.
findingDetails.impact: Enabling exact resource-gold scoring will classify declared base behavior as over-routing or mark the authored gold stale.
findingDetails.recommendation: Adjudicate base-resource legitimacy in a separate gold maintenance pass; no fix was made during this review.
findingDetails.scopeProof: All 49 expected_resources sets were compared exactly to replay output and classified by default-resource source.

results.reason: Requirements cover three modes while code exposes six.
Tasks are unchecked and committed evidence passes unscored route expectations.
Registry-router align, graph derived projection does not.
Hub index is stale and 20 of 49 packet rows fail at least one authored assertion.

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Six independent packet routers expose 49 authored intent/resource contracts and separate benchmark/runtime representations.

targetSelection.selectionReason: These surfaces form the packet-local routing producers, authored gold consumers, and deterministic execution model.

searchLedger.hypothesis: All 12 packet blind holdouts select their declared intent.

searchActions.queryOrPath: 12 holdout prompts through their packet routers
searchActions.result: 8 pass; both ClickUp and both Mobbin holdouts score no intent.

searchLedger.invariant: An out-of-domain negative loads no packet intent or resource.

searchActions.queryOrPath: negative expected none/empty to runtime zero-score branches
searchActions.result: Every runtime branch loads fallback context; five return a fallback intent label.

searchLedger.rationale: The authored suppression contract and runtime behavior disagree in every packet.

searchLedger.invariant: Deterministic replay reproduces runtime intent/resource selection.

searchActions.result: Replay drops runtime fallback intent labels and cannot see ClickUp's hardcoded fallback resource.

searchLedger.rationale: Execution paths produce different observable route contracts.

searchLedger.invariant: Legitimate universal base loads appear in positive scenario gold.

searchActions.result: 11 positive rows omit a documented always-loaded base reference.

searchLedger.rationale: Authored gold and universal loading declarations are not maintained together.
